title: ESCMID Global 2025: Gepotidacin is non-inferior to leading gonorrhoea dual therapy
summary: EAGLE-1 trial data shows gepotidacin’s potential in treating gonorrhoea, for which no new antibiotics have been approved since the 1990s.

title: Urinary incontinence linked to higher cardiovascular disease risk
summary: New research reveals a significant link between urinary incontinence and increased cardiovascular disease risk, even without reduced physical activity.

title: Live imaging reveals how viruses spread through the gut microbiome
summary: Researchers at the University of California, Irvine have developed a live-imaging system, Phollow, that tracks individual bacteriophages as they spread through the gut of zebrafish, showing that phages from different bacterial hosts vary in how they replicate, disseminate, and interact with host tissues.

title: New approach targets cell surface protein to treat leukemia and solid tumors
summary: In 2021, research led by Ryan Flynn, MD, PhD, and his mentor, Nobel laureate Carolyn Bertozzi, PhD, opened a new chapter in biology, characterizing a new kind of player on the cell surface: glycoRNAs.

title: WHO publishes first-ever guidelines for meningitis diagnosis and care
summary: According to the WHO, meningitis is a significant global health threat even though treatments and vaccines against some forms are available.

title: New recommendations for the treatment of familial Mediterranean fever
summary: In 2016, EULAR - The European Alliance of Associations for Rheumatology - published recommendations on the treatment of familial Mediterranean fever (FMF). Since then, new evidence has become available, so the recommendations have been reviewed and updated.

title: Can just 11 minutes of walking increase your lifespan? New study offers surprising insight
summary: A recent UK study reveals that just 11 minutes of daily walking reduces premature death risk. Researchers analyzed data and found even 75 minutes of weekly moderate activity lowers heart disease and cancer risks. Small changes like brisk cycling or gardening also count. This research highlights that even brief walks contribute to a healthier, longer life.

title: Statins Cut Cancer Death Risk by 61% in CLL Patients
summary: New study finds CLL/SLL patients on statins live longer. Statins may significantly reduce cancer progression and death with targeted treatments.

title: Scientists at Trinity College Dublin discover the brain’s way of making ‘cold’ memories
summary: The brain remembers ‘cold experiences’ through physical and chemical changes – called engrams – that then tell the body to make more heat.

title: Animal model of experimental H5N1 avian flu vaccine shows promise
summary: Proven effective for COVID-19, vaccine platform could be key in responding to evolving bird flu strains A developmental vaccine has shown complete protection in mouse models against the H5N1 variant of the avian influenza virus. Click to read more...
